summary:
RETEST after idempotency fix in build-static.js. All three SEO markup fixes (Bing meta, single H1 per pre-rendered file, descriptive anchors replacing 'Learn More') verified. Idempotency bug from iteration_7 is FIXED — 3 back-to-back invocations of `node build-static.js` produce byte-identical outputs; each pre-rendered file retains exactly 1

. assertSingleH1 fail-fast invariant verified via synthetic corruption (extra class="page-hero" on a non-hero

): build exits non-zero and does not overwrite files with bad markup. Full pytest suite (288 tests across 7 files) passes cleanly in a single session — the corruption cross-contamination that iteration_7 flagged is gone. Live preview URL confirmed serving updated markup (Bing meta, single

CR1

on /cr1, descriptive anchors on /).
critical_code_review_comments: normalizePageHeroHeadings correctly requires class="page-hero" in the regex so unrelated <h2>s in the home section (e.g. 'Born in the Wine Country') are no longer accidentally promoted — verified by synthetic-corruption test.

assertSingleH1 runs BEFORE fs.writeFileSync, so no partial/bad writes are possible. Because home is the first route in ROUTES, a corrupted master fails at the very first file, leaving prior outputs untouched. If the ordering ever changes, later corrupted routes would still fail-fast, but earlier successful writes would already be on disk — consider batching writes at the end (collect all rendered HTML in-memory, run assertion on all, then write) if strict all-or-nothing is desired. Non-blocking.
build is now truly idempotent — md5sum of every emitted file is identical across consecutive runs.
updated_files:
/app/backend/tests/test_seo_markup_fixes.py (updated TestLivePreview.test_preview_cr1 to accept `

CR1

` markup — the class marker introduced by the idempotency fix is now part of the pre-rendered output; previous assertion `assert "

CR1

" in r.text` was stale)
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_seo_markup_fixes.py + test_pid_urls.py + test_headers_file.py + test_redirects_file.py can now be run together in any order in a single pytest session without corrupting the pre-rendered HTML — the idempotency fix in build-static.js resolved the cross-contamination that iteration_7 documented. Master state on disk after any build: 1 <h1 class="page-hero"> (home hero) + 5 <h2 class="page-hero"> (other section heroes). normalizePageHeroHeadings self-heals both directions on every run.
rca of the issue: N/A — bug was fixed by main agent. Verification: (1) idempotency — 3 consecutive `node build-static.js` invocations produce byte-identical outputs and every pre-rendered file has exactly 1 <h1>; (2) fail-fast — adding a second class="page-hero" attribute to a non-hero <h2> in the home section causes assertSingleH1 to throw with 'expected 1, found 2' and exit code 1; (3) required-class regex — only elements literally tagged class="page-hero" are rewritten, so section headings like 'Born in the Wine Country' remain <h2>.
